title: Heliosperma pusillum

Heliosperma pusillum is a succulent perennial herb that produces small white or pink flowers. The plant is typically found growing in rocky outcrops and dry areas. Further classification includes its order, which is Saxifragales.

intro: Heliosperma pusillum, also known as Rock Jasmine or Alpine Rock Jasmine, is a low-growing, mat-forming perennial plant native to the European Alps. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which have been shown to contribute to its potential health benefits, such as reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the body. The bioactive constituents of Heliosperma pusillum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Heliosperma pusillum is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in the production of ointments and salves for topical application.
